title: Sweet Kenyan Rhubarb Tagine
description: A nutty and indulgent Kenyan dish featuring forced-rhubarb, lacquer-glaze, and dairy-free-friendly ingredients, cooked to perfection in a traditional tagine pot. This sweet and shattering dessert is a must-try for anyone looking to experience the unique flavors of Kenya.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together the forced-rhubarb, almond oil, lacquer-glaze, maple syrup, chopped almonds, coconut cream, ground cinnamon, and ground ginger.
Transfer the mixture to a traditional tagine pot.
Cover the tagine pot with a lid and bake for 45 minutes.
Remove the lid and continue baking for an additional 15 minutes, or until the rhubarb is tender and the glaze is caramelized.
Remove the tagine pot from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes.
Serve the Sweet Kenyan Rhubarb Tagine warm, garnished with additional chopped almonds if desired.
